channel.title: Government-Promises on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Government-Promises on The Guyana Brief

item.title: 🔍 [CRITICAL] Wednesday Brief: Guyanese Takes IICA Helm, Census Controversy, and Cybercrime Crackdown
item.description: Muhammad Ibrahim becomes IICA Director-General, census data sparks housing debate, cybercrime law faces criticism, and AG slams courts for ignoring legislation.

item.title: Sunday's Guyana Brief: The Mohameds Strike Back
item.description:
📊 SUNDAY QUICK STATS

🎭 THE MOHAMEDS PRESENT: “THEY’RE NOT GOING ANYWHERE” (THE MUSICAL)

This week, the Mohamed legal saga reached peak absurdity. Let’s recap the highlights:

This Week’s Timeline:

  • Monday: Acting Chief Justice says “No stay”
  • Monday (5 minutes later): Defense appeals to Full Court
  • Tuesday: Courtroom drama with first witness
  • Thursday: More drama with “late evidence”
  • Friday: Nazar’s op-ed declares “government declared war on his family”

Defense Attorney’s Battle Cry: “The Mohameds are NOT going anywhere.”

item.title: Saturday's Guyana Brief: Weekend News Circus
item.description:
📊 WEEKEND QUICK STATS

🎭 THE MOHAMEDS PLAY THE VICTIM CARD

The Mohameds are back with another performance, and this time they’re playing the victim. According to their lawyer, Sanjeev Datadin, the US wants to extradite them because they’re “Indian Guyanese” and “successful businessmen.”

Let’s Break This Down:

Their Argument: America is targeting them because of their ethnicity and success.
